value: Recently-published campaign (Check Point Research, 2026-07-06). Cavern Manticore (Iran-MOIS) modular .NET C2 framework 'Cavern'/'Cav3rn'. Samples are per-victim and near-zero-detection on VirusTotal, so prioritise behaviour (DLL side-load, anomalous AppDomain/native module loads, RMM/SysAid abuse) over hashes. No CVE is associated with this case.

value: Cavern module dispatcher: DLL names starting with 'n-' are loaded as native (LoadLibraryA); all others are managed .NET assemblies loaded via per-module AppDomain isolation (anti-forensics). Uniform get_version interface across formats.
context: Framework behavior

value: Three .NET compilation formats used as an anti-RE layer: .NET Framework (mhm/db/ode), Mixed-Mode C++/CLI (uxtheme agent), Native AOT (n-HTCommp/n-ten/n-sws). Forces multiple RE toolchains + metadata reconstruction.
context: Anti-analysis

value: Initial access via trusted IT provider and a second-hop provider (T1199); delivery via a weaponized SysAid software update / RMM / browser-based remote desktop. Lateral via RDP + SOCKS5/WebSocket tunneling; remote-print exfil when file transfer is blocked.
context: Access + delivery

value: Attribution: Iran-MOIS (Cavern Manticore) at medium confidence, based on tactical overlaps with MuddyWater and Lyceum (OilRig subgroup) plus Iranian registrar (Fars Data) and Lyceum-style victim-side proxying / XOR obfuscation. Campaign active since early 2026 against Israeli IT, government and defense.
context: Attribution
